queue.title: This Completely Changes the Way We Build Production AI Agents (Vercel Eve)
queue.description: Vercel just open sourced their new AI agent framework Eve, and now your entire production AI agent can be just a single folder of mostly markdown. Instructions are markdown, tools are TypeScript, skills and subagents are just nested folders. Eve compiles the whole thing into a durable service you deploy with one command. Durable execution, an isolated sandbox, human approvals, and evals all ship i
queue.editorial_reason: Substantive look at Vercel's new Eve agent framework and its markdown-first architecture for production agents.

queue.title: Granola's Chris Pedregal on Building a $1.5B AI Company
queue.description:
“Running a startup is a knife fight whether things are going well or not,” says Chris Pedregal, cofounder and CEO of Granola. Granola recently raised a $125 million series C round at a $1.5 billion valuation on the strength of its AI meeting notetaker.
That valuation hasn’t made Pedregal complacent. Granola built its name as the first to make good AI meeting notes, but Notion, OpenAI, and Zoom hav
queue.editorial_reason: Candid founder interview on building Granola to $1.5B, real operating lessons for bootstrapped/scaling teams.

queue.title: She Staffed Her Profitable Startup With AI Agents | Claire Vo, ChatPRD
queue.description: Claire Vo runs ChatPRD, an AI "Chief Product Officer" for over 100k product managers as the only full-time human, bootstrapped, with zero venture capital. The rest of her company is a staff of nine named AI agents she manages like real employees. (One of them emailed us 90 minutes before she arrived to coordinate scheduling.) She and Julian get into how a custom ChatGPT became a real business, why
queue.editorial_reason: Credible bootstrapped founder explains running a company with AI agent staff, concrete workflow detail.

queue.title: I Let Claude Code Run Wild. Docker Sandbox Controlled What It Could Access
queue.description:
🐳🔒 Docker Sandboxes: https://utm.io/uqScb

AI coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and other agentic coding tools can read files, run commands, install packages, and modify your project.

That makes them useful, but it also creates a serious security question:
What does your AI coding agent actually have access to?

In this video, I test Docker Sandbox with Claude Code to see whether
queue.editorial_reason: Practical security-minded workflow for sandboxing agentic coding tools, timely and actionable.

queue.title: Run Kimi K3 Inside Claude Code (MAX POWER)
queue.description:
Claude Code is one of the best coding harnesses around, and you can point it at Kimi's coding endpoint to run Kimi K3 inside it — sub-agents, skills, and loops included — while billing against a cheap, subsidized Kimi plan. Kimi even publishes its own guide for this setup, and research suggests models perform better inside Claude Code than in their native tooling.

Written Guide: https://learn.sup
queue.editorial_reason: Specific, tested workflow for running an alternative model inside Claude Code's harness at lower cost.

queue.title: Local AI models explained: How to run a fleet of Mac Studios and GPUs at home
queue.description: Alex Finn is an AI builder, YouTuber, and the creator of Vibe Code Academy, a community for people learning to build with AI tools. He’s spent five months figuring out which local models belong on
queue.editorial_reason: Detailed walkthrough of a real local AI compute setup across multiple machines, concrete and reproducible.

pool.title: I Vibecoded This Feature Using Codex

Jack joins Austin Tedesco and Kate Lee to walk through the research, internal buy-in, guardrails, implementation, and analytics behin
